vision.one_line: A calm local-first reader/search/navigation surface for large filesystem knowledge vaults.
vision.north_star: Mega Vault Viewer is the calm, local-first read/view/search surface for filesystem knowledge vaults where source files stay canonical and runtime indexes are disposable.
vision.promise: Open a vault once, keep it remembered, browse the real top-level structure, jump through daily notes, and read rich Markdown/CAS media from canonical files without preview duplicates or noisy app chrome.

principles.title: Agent-operable local vault surface
principles.rule: Core contracts, runtime expectations, and verification evidence should be structured enough for future agents to operate from a fresh clone.

principles.title: JSON and JSONL contracts first
principles.rule: Machine-consumable project/workflow contracts should be JSON or JSONL; Markdown is useful narrative context, not the only source of truth.

principles.title: Vault files remain canonical
principles.rule: Mega Vault Viewer may index, render, and cache rebuildable runtime state, but source files and CAS blobs remain the truth.
bad: runtime state as source of truth

principles.title: Calm reader shell
principles.rule: Large vault navigation and long notes need independent scroll regions, top-level folder navigation, remembered vaults, and non-redundant headers.
bad: flat dump of deep generated folders

non_goals: Duplicate binary payload or preview-file store
Broad write automation for human daily notes
Flat dump of every nested generated folder as primary navigation

acceptance_scorecard.question: Does MVV understand the JSONL-vault daily layout?
acceptance_scorecard.pass_condition: Tests assert daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md detection and first-item preference.

acceptance_scorecard.question: Does the sidebar start from top-level vault folders?
acceptance_scorecard.pass_condition: Folder aggregation groups by top-level path instead of deep generated paths.

acceptance_scorecard.question: Is the reader shell calmer and usable for long notes?
acceptance_scorecard.pass_condition: Sidebar and document pane have independent scroll regions; header is non-redundant.

acceptance_scorecard.question: Does the app remember the current vault?
acceptance_scorecard.pass_condition: Local storage stores last vault and recent vault switcher entries.

review_checklist: Did runtime tests cover daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md and top-level folders?
Does the UI avoid duplicate Opened/status/subtitle chrome?
Does the final report distinguish release-binary build from AppImage packaging if linuxdeploy fails?
Are private screenshots/vault content excluded from public docs and assets?

maturity_model.meaning: Vision and .go contracts exist and validate.
Core layout/rendering conventions have fixture tests.
Real desktop UX has been smoke-tested against a JSONL-vault-shaped vault.

residual_risk_wording: This contract defines direction and review boundaries; desktop AppImage packaging and real UI smoke still require platform/runtime evidence when linuxdeploy or Tauri UI access is unavailable.
